page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv3 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server includes 328 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution is relatively weak, as evidenced by bottom-quartile results in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core evaluations. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform at an average level. This is demonstrated by middle-quartile scores in stress-ng multi-core tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also average, with read-only large block speeds reaching 90.49 GB/sec and write speeds at 45.96 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20s_v3 offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require top-tier single-threaded compute performance. The high resource density of 160.0 GB RAM relative to 20 vCPUs makes it a viable option for hosting relational databases, enterprise applications, and caching layers. Benchmark data supports this fit, showing average performance in Redis SET operations at 15.43 million operations per second and PassMark database operations. It is also suitable for multi-threaded tasks like software compilation, image processing, and static web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded processing or heavy LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv3 family, featuring 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides 328 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates a performance tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, as shown by lower-tier Geekbench and stress-ng single-core scores, whereas multi-core performance and memory bandwidth consistently rank in the average tier. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the E20s_v3 is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads rather than compute-bound tasks. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, multi-threaded software compilation, and general enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 328 GB local storage. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weaker single-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v3 is an Intel-powered memory-optimized VM with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 328 GB local storage, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering high RAM capacity and balanced multi-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 10 physical cores
160.0 GB RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
328 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance paired with weaker single-core execution
Well-suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
